disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Migratory polyarthralgia — pain affects knees, ankles and wrists and moves unpredictably between joints over hours
trigger_question_examples: Which joints are affected and does the pain move around?
Tell me about where the pain is.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's mostly my knees and ankles, and my wrists a bit — and it's weird, it kind of moves around between them rather than staying in one spot.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Documented Ross River virus infection approximately 18 months ago with recurrent post-viral flares of joint pain and fatigue since
trigger_question_examples: Have you had anything like this before?
Any past medical problems or infections I should know about?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I caught that Ross River virus from mosquitoes about a year and a half ago, and ever since I get these flare-ups of joint pain and awful tiredness every now and then.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No single hot, bright-red, grossly swollen joint on self-observation; the pain is migratory and polyarticular rather than a monoarthritis
trigger_question_examples: Is any one joint bright red, hot to touch, or very swollen compared with the others?
Is it just one joint that has blown up, or is it several moving around?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's not just one — it moves around, and none of them look bright red or really swollen or hot. Maybe a little puffy, but nothing's blown up on its own.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: They all just hurt, I haven't really looked closely.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Afebrile — temperature 37.1 C on a home thermometer; no shaking chills or rigors
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever or shaking chills?
Can you check your temperature — and have you had the shivers?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I just checked and it's 37.1, so no real fever, and I haven't had the shakes or shivers or anything like that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cannot weight-bear or transfer; lives alone with high fall risk in a cluttered home and a low bed; had to crawl between rooms
trigger_question_examples: Is there anyone at home with you, and can you get around safely?
How are you managing to move around at home right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own and I can't stand or walk — I had to crawl to my bedroom. The place is a bit cluttered and my bed is low so getting up is a nightmare.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Already taken paracetamol and ibuprofen earlier today with no effect — an NSAID is already on board
trigger_question_examples: Have you taken any painkillers today, and did they help?
What have you already tried for the pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I took some paracetamol and some ibuprofen earlier but honestly they did nothing at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Crushing fatigue and diffuse myalgia accompanying the joint pain — consistent with the patient's recurrent post-viral flare pattern
trigger_question_examples: Aside from the joints, how is your energy and are your muscles sore?
Any other symptoms — tiredness, muscle aches?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm absolutely exhausted, wiped out, and my muscles ache all over like I've been beaten up. It's the same feeling I get with these flares.
